channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, KRON4

item.title: Harris: Trump 'has no idea' about women's health care
item.description: “The man calls himself the father of IVF? I mean what does that even mean?” said Harris, referencing her rival’s comments on in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KREX 5

item.title: Cissy Houston mourned by Dionne Warwick, politicians and more at longtime church
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Dionne Warwick, BeBe Winans, Darlene Love and a massive choir helped celebrate the life of gospel icon Cissy Houston during a music-filled memorial Thursday in the Duchess of Gospel’s hometown of Newark.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Menendez brothers' uncle requests DA meeting; says boys' motive was greed
item.description: Anderson believes Erik and Lyle Menendez weren't molested by their father Jose and that "justice was served.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KREX 5

item.title: Daughter claims mother's body 'misplaced' in Memphis cemetery
item.description: "They have no idea where or how she was lost, but they do not know where she is," says Georleshia Minter.
item.dc:creator: Quametra Wilborn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NOAA releases 2024-25 winter weather outlook: Here's what to expect
item.description: Depending on where you live in the U.S., this winter could bring a mix of snow and rain or unseasonably warm weather, according to climate experts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Parkland shooting judge criticizes shooter's attorneys during talk to law students
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — The former judge who presided over the trial of the 2018 Parkland high school mass murderer sharply criticized his public defenders during a presentation to Miami law students Thursday, saying they acted unprofessionally and crossed several lines while representing him.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Whooping cough is at a decade-high level in US
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Whooping cough is at its highest level in a decade for this time of year, U.S. health officials reported Thursday. There have been 18,506 cases of whooping cough reported so far, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NOAA releases winter weather outlook: What Southern California can expect
item.description: Depending on where you live in the U.S., this winter could bring a mix of snow and rain or an unseasonably warm start to the season, according to climate experts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KTLA 5

item.title: The Biden administration has canceled student loans for more than 1 million in public service jobs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A student loan cancellation program for public service workers has granted relief to more than 1 million Americans — up from just 7,000 who were approved before it was updated by the Biden administration two years ago.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google Flights adds 'Cheapest' option for travelers who just don't care how they get there
item.description: Google Flights has a new option for immediately finding the “cheapest” flight itinerary, but it may come at a slightly different cost.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Liam Payne's family releases statement as more details, autopsy results emerge in singer's death
item.description: Payne, 31, died after a fall from the third story of the Casa Sur hotel in Buenos Aires on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another during apparent training
item.description: Based on information gathered by NewsNation affiliate WDAF, it appears the bus driver may have been trying to teach the kids what to do in an emergency.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma parents and teachers sue to stop top education official's classroom Bible mandate
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A group of Oklahoma parents of public school students, teachers and ministers filed a lawsuit Thursday seeking to stop the state's top education official from forcing schools to incorporate the Bible into lesson plans for students in grades 5 through 12.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas Court of Criminal Appeals reverses judge’s decision which paused Robert Roberson’s execution
item.description: The Texas Court of Criminal Appeals has reversed an order by a Travis County judge, who issued a temporary restraining order which halted the execution of death row inmate Robert Roberson Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Juan A. Lozano, Associated Press, Christian Terry, Karen Araiza, KPRC 2

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia school shooting: Father and son, Colin and Colt Gray, indicted by grand jury
item.description: A Barrow County grand jury indicted 14-year-old Colt Gray and his father, Colin Gray, on several counts in connection to the Apalachee High School mass shooting that left four people dead in Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another
item.description: She won't be able to drive for another nine years, but the parents of a Missouri second grader say she's already had her first crash. Seven-year-old Alannah Cheffen, of Independence, said she crashed her school bus into another bus on school grounds.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, KTLA 5

item.title: Gulf War illness may increase risk for heart disease or stroke
item.description: The illness also was associated with more than double the odds of being diagnosed with diabetes and high blood pressure, according to the study, which was published Friday in the Journal of the American Heart Association.
item.dc:creator: Michael Merschel, American Heart Association News, NewsNation

item.title: Small town still healing after losing 20 young men on D-Day
item.description: On July 17, 1944, the small town of Bedford, Virginia with a population of just 3,200 would learn that 20 men died in the D-Day invasion.
item.dc:creator: Marielena Balouris, NewsNation

item.title: Liam Payne died from 'multiple traumas,' 'internal and external bleeding,' autopsy reveals
item.description: Officials in Argentina have released a cause of death for Liam Payne, the former One Direction member who died on Wednesday at the age of 31.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Uphill battles that put abortion rights on ballots are unlikely to end even if the measures pass
item.description: Voters in nine states are deciding next month whether to add the right to abortion to their constitutions, but the measures are unlikely to dramatically change access — at least not immediately.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New renderings show how A's ballpark would transform Las Vegas Strip
item.description: Extensive details have been revealed about the proposed $1.5 billion "multi-purpose, enclosed ballpark" on the Las Vegas Strip that could become home to the Las Vegas Athletics MLB franchise in time for the 2028 baseball season.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KRON4

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, ‘South Pacific’ star, dies at 93
item.description: Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Liam Payne, former One Direction member, dies at 31 in Argentina hotel fall
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Former One Direction singer Liam Payne, 31, whose chart-topping British boy band generated a global following of swooning fans, was found dead Wednesday after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, local officials said.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and KAITLYN HUAMANI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man and woman kidnapped, forced to withdraw money from ATMs in Chicago
item.description: A man and a woman were kidnapped at gunpoint and forced to withdraw money from ATMs overnight, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Harris, Bret Baier spar over immigration at outset of Fox News interview
item.description: Vice President Harris on Wednesday sparred at length with Fox News anchor Bret Baier over immigration in a rare sit-down with the network. Baier opened the interview by asking Harris how many immigrants had crossed the southern border during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dollar General employee finds puppies dumped in trash amid cold Arkansas weather
item.description: Three puppies, less than a week old, were abandoned this week inside of a Dollar General Market dumpster, according to Arkansas authorities.
item.dc:creator: Elena Ramirez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Universal Orlando announces Epic Universe opening date
item.description: The theme park will feature more than 50 attractions, as well as entertainment, dining and shopping options across five “expansive worlds.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: Catholic Archdiocese of L.A. to pay $880 million in childhood sex abuse claims
item.description: A landmark settlement has been reached with the Catholic Archdiocese of Los Angeles to settle more than 1,300 claims of childhood sexual abuse and assault.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Former Las Vegas-area official sentenced in murder of journalist
item.description: The former public administrator was accused of killing Jeff German after the reporter wrote articles critical of his conduct in office.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, ABC 4

item.title: California health care workers get a pay bump under a new minimum wage law
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Some of the lowest-paid health care workers in California will get a pay bump Wednesday under a state law gradually increasing their wages to at least $25 an hour.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The department will soon implement a "final click to cancel rule," a move that will require sellers to make it "as easy for consumers to cancel their enrollment as it was to sign up.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Petitions for union representation doubled under Biden's presidency, first increase since 1970s
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There has been a doubling of petitions by workers to have union representation during President Joe Biden's administration, according to figures released Tuesday by the National Labor Relations Board.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4
